title: Kamalā Sādhana
century: Medieval (c. 10th–14th c.); modern digests c. 19th–20th c.
description: The tenth of the Daśa Mahāvidyās, Kamalā ("She of the Lotus") — also called Kamalātmikā — is the tantric form of Lakṣmī: golden-complexioned, seated on a lotus, bathed by four elephants with their trunks. The benevolent, prosperity-bestowing form among the Mahāvidyās. Attested in the *Kamalā Mahāvidyā* (Goswami Prahlad Giri), *Śākta Praamoda*, *Daśa Mahāvidyā Tantra-Sāra*, and the *Mahābhāgavata P
